title: What's the point of "favorites" if they're not readily available in the new UI?
excerpt:
The new UI finally rolled out for my account and I was hunting for the “favorites” section that I (and the rest of my team) used to navigate Monday. 
Now that option seems all but pointless if it requires a multi-level p…

posts.author: Vtab (Vero Tabares)
posts.cookedHtml:
The new UI finally rolled out for my account and I was hunting for the “favorites” section that I (and the rest of my team) used to navigate Monday.

Now that option seems all but pointless if it requires a multi-level process to navigate to.

Is there a point to the favorites anymore?

posts.author: PHD (Patty)
posts.cookedHtml: I am having the same challenge. Why take away something that simplified navigation?

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml:
I agree, the “favorite boards” should be its own section in the sidebar, or a dropdown menu from the top bar.

One thing that could be at play is with the various “Products” now, where they each isolate workspaces further.

posts.author: AdamCPU (Adam Raphael)
posts.cookedHtml: I’d suggest favourites being added as buttons to the top bar, similar to a browser favourites bar, then a favourite is just a single click away.

posts.author: AsgerAH (Asger Andersen Høybye)
posts.cookedHtml: Came here for this question. Needs to be readily available from the sidebar at all times

posts.author: asktiedavis (Tieshena Davis)
posts.cookedHtml:

I’ve been asking them to fix these for the past few years.

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml: I’ve gotten around this by… making critical boards actual browser favorites!
